concept: Test / Apply a Defined Procedure to Gather Evidence
description: To carry out a defined procedure under stated conditions in order to gather evidence about a claim, function, property, or criterion. A test should distinguish its method, object, conditions, observations, interpretation, and limits. Testing does not itself prove a claim, approve a result, establish safety, certify a person or artifact, or guarantee that the tested conditions represent future use.
sound_symbolism: Opening 'so' gathers attention around the observed trial, 'ma' marks a deliberate intervention, and final 'ki' gives the procedure and criterion a clean edge. The form ends in inquiry rather than certainty.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia ha rolu somaki.' (I test the wheel.) The event-noun rule supplies a test. State the procedure, conditions, criterion, observations, and uncertainty where relevant. Distinct from 'nilaki' (inspect), 'somela' (observe), 'kisome' (assess), 'tukiro' (review), and 'masuro' (calibrate).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Repairable tools and shared infrastructure require tests whose conditions and limits remain visible to maintainers and users.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Separating a test from proof, approval, and safety discourages speakers from overstating what one procedure can establish.

tags.cognition: gathering bounded evidence about a claim or criterion
